source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Hey Shavon, what's up? You seem troubled.

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I am. I'm just having a hard time and needed someone to talk to.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Of course, man. I'm always here for you. What's going on?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: It's just everything. Work is stressing me out, my relationship is falling apart, and I feel like I'm losing touch with my friends. I don't know what to do.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Well, let's start with work then. What's going on there?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: It's just that everything is so demanding and I can't keep up. I'm constantly behind and it feels like I'm never going to catch up.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Okay, that does sound pretty tough. But it sounds like you're doing the best you can under the circumstances. Maybe you just need to cut yourself some slack and focus on one thing at a time. As for your relationship, what's going on there?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: We're just fighting all the time and it feels like we're growing apart instead of together. I don't know if we can make it through this rough patch.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Do you still love her?

source_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
source_dialogue.text: Yes, I do. But it's just so hard right now.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
source_dialogue.text: Then you should definitely try to work things out. It sounds like you're going through a lot right now, but your friends are still here for you. So don't forget that.

scene.narrative_en: Rylea sat down with Shavon and asked him what was wrong. Shavon told Rylea that he was having a hard time and needed someone to talk to. Rylea said that he would be happy to be there for his friend.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 되게 사소한 일인데 머릿속에서 떠나지가 않아요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Shavon
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 나 혼자 끙끙대는 것보다 나눠 주는 게 낫다고 생각했어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 그래서 그 이야기를 좀 더 자세히 해주고 싶어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Shavon
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 요즘 그게 정말 마음에 걸리더라고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 솔직히 말하면 기분이 많이 복잡해요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Shavon
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 오늘 하루 종일 그 생각만 하고 있었어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 그런 상황이 생기면 어떻게 해야 할지 모르겠더라고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Shavon
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 가끔은 친구한테 털어놓고 싶어질 때가 있어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 예전 같으면 그냥 넘어갔을 텐데 이번에는 좀 다르더라고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Shavon
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 아직 정리가 잘 안 돼서 횡설수설할 수도 있어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 생각해 보니 내가 먼저 이야기를 꺼낸 적이 없었더라고요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue.text: 되게 사소한 일인데 머릿속에서 떠나지가 않아요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Rylea
korean_dialogue.text: 예전 같으면 그냥 넘어갔을 텐데 이번에는 좀 다르더라고요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Shavon
korean_dialogue.text: 아직 정리가 잘 안 돼서 횡설수설할 수도 있어요.

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel
source_dialogue.text: Hi there! Are you looking for someone to take care of your pet while you're out of town?

source_dialogue.speaker: Client
source_dialogue.text: Yes, I am. I'm going on vacation for a week and I need someone to watch my cat.

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel
source_dialogue.text: That's no problem at all! I love taking care of animals, and I have experience with cats. I'll be happy to take care of your cat while you're gone.

source_dialogue.speaker: Client
source_dialogue.text: That sounds great. How much do you charge?

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel
source_dialogue.text: I charge $20 per day, plus an additional $10 per day if you want me to stay overnight.

source_dialogue.speaker: Client
source_dialogue.text: That sounds reasonable. I'll definitely need you to stay overnight, so it will be $30 per day total. When can you start?

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel
source_dialogue.text: Any time that works for you! Just let me know when you'll be leaving and when you'll be back, and I'll take care of everything in between.

source_dialogue.speaker: Client
source_dialogue.text: Alright, I'll be leaving on Saturday and I'll be back next Saturday. Can you come over on Friday evening to meet my cat and get everything set up?

source_dialogue.speaker: Jabriel
source_dialogue.text: Yes, that sounds perfect. I'll see you on Friday at 6pm.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Friendly and enthusiastic, uses polite offers.
Polite and direct, asks practical questions.

scene.narrative_en: Jabriel provides a service by taking care of people's pets while they are out of town. Jabriel is a helpful person who loves animals, so she decided to start this business. She has been taking care of pets for three years and has never had any problems.

source_dialogue.speaker: Danna
source_dialogue.text: Hey, Mattea. What's up?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mattea
source_dialogue.text: Not much, what's up with you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Danna
source_dialogue.text: Just hanging out, same old same old. So, what's new with you?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mattea
source_dialogue.text: Well, I've been thinking about what you said the other day.

source_dialogue.speaker: Danna
source_dialogue.text: What did I say?

source_dialogue.speaker: Mattea
source_dialogue.text: You know, about how I should stand up to my dad and stop letting him control my life.

source_dialogue.speaker: Danna
source_dialogue.text: Oh yeah, that.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mattea
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I've been thinking about it a lot and I've decided that you're right. I'm going to start standing up for myself more and not let him control me anymore.

source_dialogue.speaker: Danna
source_dialogue.text: That's great, Mattea! I'm really proud of you for making that decision.

source_dialogue.speaker: Mattea
source_dialogue.text: Thanks, Danna. It's not going to be easy, but I'm determined to do it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Danna
source_dialogue.text: You can do it, Mattea. I know you can.

scene.narrative_en: Danna always shares Mattea's point of view because he is loyal to his friend. Danna never questions Mattea's decisions and always stands by his side, no matter what the situation is.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Danna
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 아직 정리가 잘 안 돼서 횡설수설할 수도 있어요.

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: I know what you're thinking, Dontrell. I can sense your feelings and know exactly what you're going to say or do next. You're thinking about how much you miss your family, and how you wish you could see them again. You're wondering if there's anything you can do to make that happen. And you're also feeling a little guilty, because you know that if you could see them again, it would only be for a short while before they died.

source_dialogue.speaker: Dontrell
source_dialogue.text: Cornell, how can you possibly know all that? It's like you can read my mind!

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: I told you, I can sense your feelings. It's like I have a connection to your thoughts and emotions. I'm not sure why or how it works, but ever since we met, I've been able to understand what you're thinking and feeling pretty easily.

source_dialogue.speaker: Dontrell
source_dialogue.text: Wow...that's amazing! And a little bit scary too. But it does explain why we've always been so close - even though we've only known each other for a few months.

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, I guess our connection is pretty strong. But don't worry, Dontrell - I'll never use it to manipulate or control you in any way. I promise."

source_dialogue.speaker: Dontrell
source_dialogue.text: That's a relief! I was starting to feel a little bit like you could read my mind, and it was kind of creepy. But if you promise not to use your abilities to control me, then I'm okay with it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Cornell
source_dialogue.text: I swear, Dontrell. I'll never use my abilities to control or manipulate you in any way. You have my word.

speakers.speech_style_notes: Steady, reassuring tone with measured pacing.

scene.narrative_en: Cornell knows what Dontrell is thinking. He can sense her feelings and know exactly what she is going to say or do next.

source_dialogue.speaker: Bram
source_dialogue.text: Hi, God. I'm feeling a little anxious about my math test tomorrow. I'm just not sure if I'm going to be able to do it. Can you give me some guidance?

source_dialogue.speaker: God
source_dialogue.text: Of course, Bram. I will always be here for you. Just remember that I am with you and that I will help you through anything. You can do this, Bram. Just have faith and believe in yourself.

source_dialogue.speaker: Bram
source_dialogue.text: Thank you, God. Just knowing that you're with me makes me feel a lot better. I'll try my best and I know that with your help, I can do it.

source_dialogue.speaker: God
source_dialogue.text: That's the spirit, Bram! I know you can do it.

scene.narrative_en: Bram was worried about his upcoming math test. He prayed to God for help and guidance. After praying, Bram felt more confident and comforted.

mapped_refs.notes: 신은 일반적인 신성 존재를 일컫는 말입니다.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Bram
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 생각해 보니 내가 먼저 이야기를 꺼낸 적이 없었더라고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: God
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 이런 얘기는 쉽게 꺼내기 어렵잖아요, 그래도 오늘은 말해보려고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Bram
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 되게 사소한 일인데 머릿속에서 떠나지가 않아요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: God
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 나 혼자 끙끙대는 것보다 나눠 주는 게 낫다고 생각했어요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Bram
korean_dialogue.text: 생각해 보니 내가 먼저 이야기를 꺼낸 적이 없었더라고요. 특히 신 얘기가 나왔어요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: God
korean_dialogue.text: 이런 얘기는 쉽게 꺼내기 어렵잖아요, 그래도 오늘은 말해보려고요. 특히 신 얘기가 나왔어요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: God
korean_dialogue.text: 나 혼자 끙끙대는 것보다 나눠 주는 게 낫다고 생각했어요. 특히 신 얘기가 나왔어요.

source_dialogue.speaker: Tinsley
source_dialogue.text: You're asking for a lot, Tinsley. We've been partners for a long time and I think you know that we're not in a position to give you what you're asking for.

source_dialogue.speaker: CEO
source_dialogue.text: What exactly are you asking for?

source_dialogue.speaker: Tinsley
source_dialogue.text: I want a larger percentage of the profits and a say in how the product is marketed.

source_dialogue.speaker: CEO
source_dialogue.text: Why do you think you deserve that?

source_dialogue.speaker: Tinsley
source_dialogue.text: Because I'm the one who came up with the product in the first place. It's my name on it and I should be getting a bigger cut of the profits. And as for marketing, I know what works and what doesn't. I can help make sure that our product is presented in the best light possible.

source_dialogue.speaker: CEO
source_dialogue.text: Hmm...I see your point. Let me talk to the other partners and see what they think. I'll get back to you soon.

scene.narrative_en: This time, Tinsley wants a larger percentage of the profits and a say in how the product is marketed.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Tinsley
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 되게 사소한 일인데 머릿속에서 떠나지가 않아요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: CEO
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 나 혼자 끙끙대는 것보다 나눠 주는 게 낫다고 생각했어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Tinsley
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 그래서 그 이야기를 좀 더 자세히 해주고 싶어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: CEO
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 요즘 그게 정말 마음에 걸리더라고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Tinsley
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 솔직히 말하면 기분이 많이 복잡해요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: CEO
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 오늘 하루 종일 그 생각만 하고 있었어요.

source_dialogue.speaker: Rachelle
source_dialogue.text: I'm so glad you're home! I have a surprise for you.

source_dialogue.speaker: Alysson
source_dialogue.text: What is it?

source_dialogue.speaker: Rachelle
source_dialogue.text: Come inside and see!

source_dialogue.speaker: Alysson
source_dialogue.text: Okay!

source_dialogue.speaker: Alysson
source_dialogue.text: Wow! This is amazing! Thank you so much, Rachelle!

source_dialogue.speaker: Rachelle
source_dialogue.text: You're welcome. I wanted to make your birthday extra special this year.

scene.narrative_en: Rachelle had a plan to make Alysson's birthday special. She secretly invited all of Alysson's friends over and decorated the house with balloons and streamers. When Alysson came home from school, she was surprised and happy to see all her friends there waiting for her.

mapped_refs.notes: 생일은 일반적으로 축하하는 생일 행사를 의미합니다.

source_dialogue.speaker: Aariz
source_dialogue.text: Hi, Pietro. How are you doing today?

source_dialogue.speaker: Pietro
source_dialogue.text: I'm doing well, Aariz. Thanks for speaking with me.

source_dialogue.speaker: Aariz
source_dialogue.text: Of course! So, what's been going on?

source_dialogue.speaker: Pietro
source_dialogue.text: Well, I've just been really busy lately. Work has been keeping me busy and I haven't had much time for anything else.

source_dialogue.speaker: Aariz
source_dialogue.text: That sounds tough. I know how it feels to be too busy to do anything else but work. It's not fun.

source_dialogue.speaker: Pietro
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, it's not fun at all. But I'm trying to make the best of it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Aariz
source_dialogue.text: That's good! I think that's the best we can do in situations like these. Just try to keep our heads up and power through it.

source_dialogue.speaker: Pietro
source_dialogue.text: Yeah, that's what I'm trying to do. Thanks for the encouragement, Aariz

speakers.speech_style_notes: Polite, slightly formal but warm tone, uses encouraging language.
Reserved, appreciative, uses brief affirmative responses.

scene.narrative_en: Aariz spoke kindly to Pietro, and he felt his day brighten. Aariz was pleased that she could make Pietro's day better.

source_dialogue.speaker: Makennah
source_dialogue.text: So, can you tell me a little bit about the job?

source_dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
source_dialogue.text: Well, it's a customer service position. You'll be working with our clients, helping them with whatever they need.

source_dialogue.speaker: Makennah
source_dialogue.text: I see. And what are the qualifications for this job?

source_dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
source_dialogue.text: Well, we're looking for someone who is friendly and helpful, and who has experience dealing with customers.

source_dialogue.speaker: Makennah
source_dialogue.text: I definitely have those qualifications. I've been working in customer service for years, and I love helping people. I'm confident that I would be great at this job.

source_dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
source_dialogue.text: Great! We'll give you a call if we decide to move forward with your application.

scene.narrative_en: Makennah had the perfect qualifications for the job and he also had the right attitude. Makennah was very determined to get this job and he did everything possible to make sure that he would be the right person for it. In the end, Makennah got exactly what he wanted: The Job.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Makennah
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 솔직히 말하면 기분이 많이 복잡해요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Interviewer
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 오늘 하루 종일 그 생각만 하고 있었어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Makennah
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 그런 상황이 생기면 어떻게 해야 할지 모르겠더라고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Interviewer
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 가끔은 친구한테 털어놓고 싶어질 때가 있어요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Makennah
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 예전 같으면 그냥 넘어갔을 텐데 이번에는 좀 다르더라고요.

korean_dialogue_draft.speaker: Interviewer
korean_dialogue_draft.text: 이번 발화: 아직 정리가 잘 안 돼서 횡설수설할 수도 있어요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Makennah
korean_dialogue.text: 솔직히 말하면 기분이 많이 복잡해요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Interviewer

korean_dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
korean_dialogue.text: 가끔은 친구한테 털어놓고 싶어질 때가 있어요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Makennah
korean_dialogue.text: 예전 같으면 그냥 넘어갔을 텐데 이번에는 좀 다르더라고요.

korean_dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
korean_dialogue.text: 아직 정리가 잘 안 돼서 횡설수설할 수도 있어요.

source_dialogue.speaker: Veda
source_dialogue.text: Hi, Father. I'm Veda. I'm new to the area and was curious about your church. Could you tell me a little bit about it?

source_dialogue.speaker: Priest
source_dialogue.text: Of course, Veda. Our church is based on the teachings of Jesus Christ. We believe in loving our neighbor and treating others as we would want to be treated. We strive to live according to Christ's example and teachings.

source_dialogue.speaker: Veda
source_dialogue.text: That sounds like a really great way to live. I can see why so many people are drawn to this religion. What do you think makes Christianity different from other religions?

source_dialogue.speaker: Priest
source_dialogue.text: Well, there are a lot of different interpretations of Christianity, but for us, it's all about following Jesus Christ's example. He was a man who loved unconditionally and forgave easily. He preached compassion and understanding, even for those who disagreed with him or did not follow his teachings perfectly. We try to emulate that in our own lives."

source_dialogue.speaker: Veda
source_dialogue.text: That does sound different. I know that forgiveness is a big part of Christianity, but it's not always easy to do. How do you think Christians are able to forgive so easily?

source_dialogue.speaker: Priest
source_dialogue.text: Well, I think it comes from our belief that everyone is capable of change and redemption. We believe that no one is beyond hope or help, and so we are always willing to forgive those who have wronged us. It's not always easy, but it is something we strive for.

scene.narrative_en: Veda thought about going to church because she was interested in the religion. She had never been to church before, but she had heard good things about it. She decided to go to a nearby church and see what it was like.

mapped_refs.notes: Koreans typically call him 예수 그리스도 in religious contexts.
